results.rationale: Figure 1 visibly shows the complete protractor and its 1-degree subdivisions; the labelled response and 1° grading evidence match the question.
All six Table 1 angle pairs are rendered in a structured table; the diagram-capable response, level criteria, checklist, and model method support the 6-mark task.
Figure 2 provides the complete scaled graph and Table 2 visibly provides (70°, 35°) and (80°, 37°); the asset canvas and plot/curve criteria fit exactly.
Figure 2's visible plotted points show the nonlinear trend, with the preceding completion task also visible; the curve/not-straight grading point answers the prompt.
Figure 4 contains the reflector, incident ray, direction arrow, and incidence point, and is also the annotation canvas; the normal and reflected-ray criteria fit.
The local option asset visibly contains all three ray diagrams in top-to-bottom order, and the selectable labels and correct answer consistently identify the top diagram.
The visible setup states that the child is standing still and supplies Figure 5; separate force and reason fields align with the 0 N and balanced-forces marks.
The complete equations-sheet page visibly includes W = Fs, and the one-line response, checklist, and model equation all fit.
The 2.8 m distance, 35 J work, and equations sheet are visible; the working surface and 12.5 N calculation evidence are complete and consistent.
The surface change and unchanged applied force are explicit, and the model/checklist correctly link reduced resistance to greater resultant force and increasing speed.
The visible equations sheet contains M = Fd, and the response and grading evidence require that exact equation.
The prompt visibly supplies 2.0 N and 7.5 cm and the equations sheet supplies M = Fd; the conversion, working, and 0.15 N m key fit.
Figure 6 clearly shows the meshed gears, sizes, pivot, and applied force; the opposite/clockwise rotation and force-or-moment explanation are gradable from it.
The prompt clearly excludes the Sun and asks for three other solar-system object types; three labelled fields and a broad official accepted set support grading.
Table 3 visibly lists all four star masses, making Betelgeuse identifiable as the most massive; the key and model answer agree.
The gigametre value and all four metre options are visible, and 1.1 × 10¹⁷ m is both selectable and correctly keyed.
Figure 7 visibly preserves the Sun, galaxy A, and galaxy B line patterns plus blue/red direction, supporting both red-shift and relative-speed conclusions in the key.
Distance, light speed, units, and the distance-speed-time equation are all visible; the conversion and 1.2 × 10⁹ s calculation are fully graded.
The hydrogen starting point and element-production command are clear; the level evidence, indicative fusion/supernova content, checklist, and model answer cover the 4-mark response.
Figure 8 visibly establishes increasing depth below the sea surface, and the grading evidence correctly links a taller water column to increased pressure.
Pressure change, density, gravitational field strength, units, and p = hρg are visible; the response and 10 940 m grading route are complete.
All four P/S wave-type statements are visible and selectable, with the longitudinal-P/transverse-S option keyed exactly.
Figure 9 clearly shows the earthquake, liquid outer core, and positions A–D; the mixed choice-plus-reason control and D/liquid-wave grading dependency fit.
Frequency, wave speed, units, and v = fλ are visible; the conversion and 1250 m answer are consistently supported.
The prompt explicitly states the relative P- and S-wave speeds and arrival-time difference; the positive and directly proportional relationship is clearly gradable.
Figure 10 visibly labels force F along the string, making tension identifiable; the one-line response and key match.
The investigation aim and complete trolley-hanger apparatus are visible, and the accepted combined/system mass control variable is supported by the checklist and model answer.
Starting from rest, constant acceleration, measured distance, and elapsed time are explicit; the response space and accepted calculation routes support all three marks.
All three acceleration readings and units are visible; the half-range method gives ±0.03 m/s² and fits the labelled response and marking evidence.
Figure 11 visibly shows the trolley pulled down the raised runway with unchanged 2.0 N force; the weight-component and increased-acceleration explanation fits.
The excluded shared property is explicit, three labelled fields are available, and the official accepted similarity set plus model answer can grade three distinct responses.
The medical-use prompt is self-contained, and the accepted imaging, treatment, and sterilisation evidence plus model answer make the one-mark response gradable.
The comparison is explicit and needs no external source; the ionisation contrast and DNA/cell-harm link supply complete two-mark grading evidence.
The specific-frequency production question is self-contained; the oscillating-charge/circuit mechanism and matching wave frequency are covered by the checklist and model answer.
Figure 12 provides the complete velocity-time axes, scales, and line through 600 s; the triangle-plus-rectangle area route and 27 440 m result match it.
The two visibly different deceleration gradients in Figure 12 support the smaller post-720 s deceleration and braking force stated in the marking evidence.
Figure 12 visibly supplies the steepest section's 56 m/s, 20 m/s, 600 s, and 720 s readings; the 0.3 m/s² gradient key fits.
Speed, braking force, mass, and the required equations are visible; the ample working surface and official force, energy, and momentum routes all reach 1600 m.
The alcohol and train-driving scenario is explicit; the reaction-time, thinking-distance, and total-stopping-distance links provide complete three-mark grading evidence.
Figure 13 and the stated +0.4 mA transient visibly establish the moving-wire setup; generator effect/electromagnetic induction is correctly keyed.
Figure 13 shows the wire, magnetic field apparatus, ammeter, and closed circuit, while the movement and reading are stated; all three induction links are gradable.
The original +0.4 mA reading, unchanged motion, reversed field, and all three choices are visible; −0.4 mA is selectable and correctly keyed.
Figure 14 clearly labels the sound wave, diaphragm, attached coil, magnet, and circuit connections; the vibration-to-induced-AC sequence fits the three-mark evidence.

rationale: All 43 assembled contexts are learner-solvable from the visible text, structured tables, prior subparts, response surfaces, and inspected local assets. Every response control is renderable, every fixed answer is selectable, and the mark-scheme evidence and model answers fit the visible questions; no repairs are required.
